indicators.label: RBI Repo Rate
indicators.source: Reserve Bank of India — MPC Statement June 5, 2026 (unanimous hold)
indicators.interpretation: MPC voted unanimously to hold repo rate at 5.25% at June 5 meeting, retaining neutral stance. Cumulative 100 bps of cuts since Feb 2025 (prev 5.50%). RBI raised FY27 CPI projection to 5.1% and cut GDP forecast to 6.6%, citing West Asia conflict energy risks and sub-normal monsoon. With Brent now at $74.26/bbl (June 30), well below the war peak, the case for an August 6-8 MPC cut has strengthened. SDF: 5.00%; MSF/Bank rate: 5.50%.

indicators.label: CPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) — CPI Press Release May 2026 via PIB
indicators.interpretation: CPI at 3.93% in May 2026 (provisional) remains just below the RBI's 4% midpoint target. Food inflation (CFPI) climbed to 4.78%; rural CPI 4.25%, urban 3.53%. The first fuel price hike in four years swung transport inflation from -0.01% to +1.75%. June CPI data is scheduled for release July 13, 2026. With Brent crude now at ~$74.26/bbl on June 30, import-driven inflation pressure remains easing, suggesting June CPI may moderate back toward or below 3.5%.

indicators.label: WPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Commerce & Industry — WPI Press Release May 2026 (new 2022-23 base series); LiveMint / Business Standard
indicators.interpretation: WPI at 9.68% in May 2026 (new 2022-23 base series, 957 items) is a series high, driven by war-era crude: Fuel & Power +30.33% YoY; crude petroleum +61.51% YoY; manufactured products +7.48%. With Brent now at $74.26/bbl on June 30 — a ~40% decline from the conflict peak — the June WPI (due mid-July) is expected to moderate sharply. India has also introduced a Producer Price Index (PPI) to eventually replace WPI. WPI is a lagging indicator of a crisis that has substantially resolved.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY26 (Provisional Actual)
indicators.source: MoSPI — Provisional Estimates of National Income FY2025-26 (released June 5, 2026); Business Standard
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 real GDP grew 7.7%, the fastest in three years, beating consensus of 7.2-7.5%. Q4 FY26 clocked 7.8% YoY. Manufacturing +10.7%; financial services and real estate +10.4%; trade, hotels, transport +11%. GFCF +10.8% in Q4 signals investment-led momentum. India Inc Q4 FY26 aggregate net profit grew 25.3% YoY. Real GDP at constant prices: ₹323.12 lakh crore in FY26 vs ₹299.89 lakh crore in FY25. Nominal GDP: ₹346.36 lakh crore (+8.9% YoY). This strong FY26 base provides a solid platform for FY27.

indicators.label: GDP Growth Q4 FY26 (Jan-Mar 2026)
indicators.source: MoSPI — Provisional Estimates of National Income FY2025-26 (released June 5, 2026); Business Standard
indicators.interpretation: Q4 FY26 GDP grew 7.8% YoY (Jan-Mar 2026), comfortably beating consensus of 7.2% (Reuters poll of 45 economists) and surpassing the RBI's own Q4 estimate of 7.0%. Q3 FY26 was 8.0%. Manufacturing grew 10.7% in Q4; services +9%+. Real GDP at constant prices: ₹87.77 lakh crore in Q4 FY26 vs ₹81.40 lakh crore a year earlier. Nominal GDP for the quarter: ₹94.65 lakh crore (+9.1% YoY). The strong Q4 print limits downside risk to FY27 growth despite the 6.6% RBI forecast floor.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY27 RBI Forecast
indicators.source: Moneycontrol — RBI MPC Minutes: FY27 growth projected at 6.6%
indicators.interpretation: RBI's official FY27 GDP forecast was revised down to 6.6% (from 6.9%) at the June MPC, citing West Asia conflict energy risks and sub-normal monsoon. With Brent now at $74.26/bbl on June 30 — well below the war-peak levels — the actual FY27 outcome is likely to track the 6.8-7.0% range, making 6.6% a conservative floor. Monsoon cumulative deficit of -40% of LPA for full June remains a risk factor for kharif sowing and rural demand.

indicators.label: IIP Industrial Production Growth (May 2026)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol — India's industrial output grows 5.1% in May (MoSPI)
indicators.interpretation: Industrial output grew 5.1% YoY in May 2026 (released June 29 by MoSPI under the new 2022-23 base series). Manufacturing +5.5% (largest weight); electricity & gas supply +9.9%; but mining & quarrying contracted -1.6%. May IIP beat the consensus estimate of 4.2% and improved from April's 4.9%. The sustained manufacturing momentum and strong electricity output signal healthy industrial activity despite weakness in energy extraction.

indicators.label: Eight Core Sector Growth (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Commerce & Industry — Index of Eight Core Industries May 2026; Economic Times
indicators.interpretation: Core sector (8 industries, ~40% of IIP) grew just 0.5% YoY in May 2026 — a 7-month low, down from 1.8% in April. Five of eight sectors contracted: coal -9.3%, crude oil -4.6%, natural gas -4.9%, refinery products -8.7%, fertiliser -0.9%. Growth supported only by electricity +8.7%, cement +8.4%, and steel +5.0%. Cumulative April-May FY27 core sector: +1.1% YoY. The sharp refinery products contraction is largely war-era disruption in crude supply chains, expected to normalise as Brent stabilises below $75/bbl.

indicators.label: FII/FPI Net Cash (June 29, 2026 — Provisional, NSE+BSE+MSEI)
indicators.source: NSE India — FII/DII Provisional Cash Market Data June 29, 2026 (NSE+BSE+MSEI)
indicators.interpretation: FIIs net sold ₹1,350.10 Cr on June 29 (NSE+BSE+MSEI provisional). June 30 provisional data was not yet released on NSE at the time of this refresh. June MTD FII net: -₹46,472 Cr through June 29. However, FPI debt inflows for June 2026 were a record ₹53,363 Cr (NSDL via The Hindu, June 29), effectively offsetting the equity selling and resulting in the first month of net positive FPI overall inflow in 2026 (combined equity + debt: +~₹334 Cr net).

indicators.label: FII Net Equity Flows (June 2026 MTD through June 29)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol FII/DII activity table (NSE+BSE+MSEI cash, month-to-date through June 29)
indicators.interpretation: FII June MTD net equity: -₹46,472 Cr through June 29 (Moneycontrol). Gross purchases: ₹3,16,847 Cr; Gross sales: ₹3,63,318 Cr. However, FPI June debt inflows were a record ₹53,363 Cr (NSDL), the highest ever monthly debt inflow, driven by India-US tax treaty benefits and JP Morgan/Bloomberg index inclusion. Combined FPI equity + debt for June 2026: approximately net positive ~₹334 Cr — first positive overall month in 2026. Catalyst for further equity re-entry: August 6-8 RBI MPC cut and India-US bilateral trade agreement.

indicators.label: DII Net Flows (June 2026 MTD through June 29)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol FII/DII activity table (NSE+BSE+MSEI cash, month-to-date through June 29)
indicators.interpretation: DII June MTD net: +₹78,958 Cr through June 29 — a strong acceleration from +₹76,156 Cr through June 25. June 29 DII net: +₹2,801.45 Cr (NSE+BSE+MSEI). Monthly SIP inflows structurally firm at ~₹31,000+ Cr/month, providing a reliable demand floor that has consistently absorbed FII equity selling throughout 2026. DII YTD net purchases substantially exceed FII equity outflows, confirming domestic institutional investors as the primary market price-setter. June 30 being monthly F&O expiry day (last Tuesday of June), DII activity may be elevated.

indicators.label: DII Net Cash (June 29, 2026 — Provisional, NSE+BSE+MSEI)
indicators.source: NSE India — FII/DII Provisional Cash Market Data June 29, 2026 (NSE+BSE+MSEI)
indicators.interpretation: DIIs net bought ₹2,801.45 Cr on June 29 (NSE+BSE+MSEI provisional). June 30 provisional data was not yet released on NSE at the time of this refresh. Combined institutional net on June 29: approximately +₹1,451 Cr, mildly positive (DII +₹2,801 vs FII -₹1,350). DII buying, driven by mutual fund SIP inflows and insurance flows, continues to act as the structural demand floor. June 30 is monthly F&O expiry (last Tuesday of June under post-2025 NSE calendar); institutional activity may be elevated as positions are unwound.

indicators.label: FPI Debt Inflows (June 2026 — Record Monthly)
indicators.source: DNA — no approved-domain source at refresh
indicators.interpretation: NSDL data indicated a record monthly FPI debt inflow (~Rs 53,363 cr) in June 2026, but this was only available from a non-approved domain at refresh time, so it is marked DNA rather than cited from an unapproved source.

indicators.label: USD/INR Exchange Rate
indicators.source: Perplexity finance connector (real-time, USDINR=X)
indicators.interpretation: USD/INR rate was 94.57 per finance connector at ~14:05 UTC on June 30 (prev close 94.53, change +0.04 / +0.04%). Day's range: 94.52–94.75. The rupee has strengthened substantially from the ~96.8 war peak in May as Brent has fallen to $74.26/bbl. On a quarter-end basis (Q1 FY27 close), the rupee is ~2.2% stronger vs the May-2026 crisis peak. Continued rupee stability reduces import inflation, supports RBI's CAD management, and is broadly positive for macro. Mild headwind for IT and pharma dollar exporters, but materially supportive for energy importers and overall macro balance.

indicators.label: Brent Crude Oil (USD/bbl)
indicators.source: Perplexity finance connector (real-time, BZ=F)
indicators.interpretation: Brent crude at $74.26/bbl on June 30 (finance connector, +$0.35 / +0.47% from prev close $73.91; day range $73.08–$74.83). Brent has fallen ~40% from the $124/bbl conflict peak and ~$5/bbl lower than mid-June's $79.34. At $74.26/bbl, India's crude import bill is dramatically reduced versus war-peak levels — the single most powerful macro tailwind for India's current account deficit, CPI, and monetary policy. On a Q1 FY27 quarter-end basis (today), Brent is $74.26 vs ~$80/bbl at the start of April FY27. This level makes August RBI rate cut the near-certain base case and is strongly positive for Indian equities broadly.

indicators.label: India 10Y G-Sec Yield (Benchmark: 6.94% GS 2036)
indicators.source: CCIL (Clearing Corporation of India Ltd) — Tenorwise Indicative Yields, 9Y-10Y bucket, June 29, 2026
indicators.interpretation: Latest CCIL (approved Tier 1 source) reading for the 10Y benchmark (6.94% GS 2036): 6.7593% as of June 29, 2026. June 30 EOD data was not yet posted on CCIL at refresh time. The yield has declined ~23 bps since the June 5 MPC decision (was ~6.99% post-MPC). Business Standard confirmed 6.77% on June 25 and 6.80% on June 24 as prior reference points. The prevailing trend is firmly downward: five consecutive sessions of decline to a fourteen-week low, driven by record FPI debt inflows (₹53,363 Cr in June), stable Brent, and growing August MPC rate-cut conviction. Lower yields support NBFC, realty, and capital goods re-rating.

indicators.label: Fiscal Deficit FY26 (Provisional Actuals)
indicators.source: Controller General of Accounts (CGA) — FY26 Provisional Actuals; Economic Times
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 fiscal deficit: ₹15.19 lakh crore (4.4% of GDP), meeting the government's revised target. Achieved by slashing total expenditure ~₹60,000 Cr below revised estimates (capex cut ₹33,055 Cr; revenue exp cut ₹26,636 Cr). For FY27, the government's budget target remains 4.4% of GDP (Budget 2026-27). April FY27 fiscal deficit: 21.4% of annual target — seasonal and expected. Sustained fiscal discipline is positive for sovereign rating trajectory and bond market confidence.

indicators.label: Fiscal Deficit FY27 (April 2026 — 21.4% of Target)
indicators.source: Controller General of Accounts / LiveMint — India fiscal deficit April 2026 FY27
indicators.interpretation: India's central government fiscal deficit touched 21.4% of the FY27 budget estimate in April 2026 — a seasonal pattern at the start of the financial year. The FY27 budget fiscal deficit target is 4.4% of GDP (~₹17.4 lakh crore per Budget 2026-27). With Brent now at $74.26, the energy risk to the fiscal has materially diminished. Direct tax collections: +14.6% YoY to ₹5.21 lakh crore through June 17, providing confidence in revenue trajectory.

indicators.label: GST Collections (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Finance — GST Collections May 2026 (released June 1); Moneycontrol / Economic Times
indicators.interpretation: Gross GST in May 2026: ₹1,94,184 crore (+3.2% YoY vs ₹1,88,172 Cr in May 2025). Moderation from April's record ₹2.42 lakh crore is seasonal. CGST: ₹37,397 Cr; SGST: ₹45,143 Cr; IGST: ₹51,990 Cr. April-May combined: ₹4.37 lakh crore (+6.2% YoY). June 2026 GST data is due July 1, 2026 — not yet released at the time of this refresh.

indicators.label: GST Collections (June 2026)
indicators.source: DNA — June 2026 GST data due July 1, 2026; not yet released
indicators.interpretation: June 2026 GST collections data is scheduled for release on July 1, 2026 and is not yet available at the time of this refresh. Marked DNA. May 2026 print (₹1.94 lakh crore, +3.2% YoY) remains the latest available monthly figure.

indicators.label: Southwest Monsoon 2026 — Progress & IMD Forecast (June 30)
indicators.value: Full June 2026 cumulative All-India rainfall: 99.5 mm vs LPA of 165.3 mm; deficit -40% of LPA. NLM at Surat/Indore/Mandla/Daltonganj/Motihari/28.3°N-83°E (as of June 29 IMD morning bulletin). Central India worst hit at ~50% deficit.
indicators.prev: NLM June 29 morning bulletin position; cumulative -42% LPA through June 24
indicators.source: IMD — Monsoon Information / rainfall bulletin (official)
indicators.interpretation: Full June 2026 cumulative All-India rainfall: 99.5 mm vs normal 165.3 mm — a deficit of -40% of LPA for the entire month of June (India Today, June 30, 2026, citing IMD data). Central India is the worst-hit zone with a ~50% deficit. IMD's updated long-range forecast projects the 2026 southwest monsoon season rainfall at 90% of LPA (below-normal, released May 29). The NLM (as of IMD June 29 morning bulletin, last available from mausam.imd.gov.in) continued to pass through Surat, Indore, Mandla, Daltonganj, Motihari, and 28.3°N/83°E. Continued deficiency risks food inflation, rural FMCG and two-wheeler volumes, agri-NBFC credit quality, and delayed kharif sowing across Central/Northwest India.

marketState.headline: Nifty at 23,866 intraday (-0.34%) on monthly F&O expiry day; Brent at $74.26/bbl; FPI debt inflows at record ₹53,363 Cr for June; 10Y G-sec yield at 14-week low ~6.76%.

boosting.detail: Brent closes Q1 FY27 at $74.26/bbl — a ~40% collapse from the $124 conflict peak. This is the single largest macro relief for India's CAD, CPI, and monetary policy trajectory. August 6-8 RBI rate cut is now the near-certain base case.

boosting.factor: Record FPI debt inflow: ₹53,363 Cr in June 2026
boosting.detail: First month of positive net overall FPI flows (equity + debt combined: +~₹334 Cr) in 2026. Record debt inflow driven by India-US tax treaty, JP Morgan/Bloomberg index inclusion, and attractive real yields. Signals institutional macro re-rating of India.

Five consecutive sessions of yield decline to a 14-week low. CCIL June 29 reading: 6.7593%. Down ~23 bps since June 5 MPC decision. Lower cost of capital supports NBFC, realty, and capex re-rating ahead of August MPC.

boosting.factor: DII structural buying (+₹78,958 Cr June MTD through June 29)
boosting.detail: Domestic institutions provide a strong demand floor. Monthly SIP inflows structurally firm. June MTD DII buying accelerating — final June 30 (monthly expiry) data not yet released.

boosting.factor: GDP Q4 FY26 at 7.8% — a strong quarterly close
boosting.detail: Q4 FY26 GDP beat consensus (7.2% Reuters poll) at 7.8%. FY26 full year 7.7%. India Inc Q4 FY26 net profit +25.3% YoY (9-quarter high). Strong base reduces FY27 downside risk.
CPI at 3.93% (May), below RBI's 4% midpoint target. FY26 fiscal deficit met at 4.4% of GDP. Direct tax collections +14.6% YoY through June 17. Macro credibility intact.

lagging.factor: FII equity selling persistence — June MTD -₹46,472 Cr
lagging.detail: FII equity net -₹46,472 Cr in June MTD through June 29. Monthly F&O expiry day (June 30) could see elevated FII derivatives-linked selling. Equity re-entry catalyst pending: August RBI cut + India-US BTA finalisation.

lagging.factor: Monsoon June 2026 deficit: -40% of LPA (full month)
lagging.detail: Full June 2026 All-India rainfall: 99.5 mm vs 165.3 mm normal — -40% deficit. Central India worst hit (~50% deficit). IMD projects full season at 90% of LPA (below normal). Risk to food inflation, rural demand, kharif sowing.

Eight core sectors grew just 0.5% in May — coal -9.3%, refinery products -8.7%, crude oil -4.6%. Investment cycle momentum uneven; energy extraction disruption from conflict lingers but should normalise.

lagging.factor: WPI at 9.68% (series high — lagging war-era artifact)
lagging.detail: War-era crude spike pushed WPI to a new series high in May. Expected to moderate sharply in the June print (due mid-July) as Brent has fallen to $74.26. Lagging indicator of a crisis substantially resolved.

lagging.factor: Nifty -0.34% intraday on monthly F&O expiry (June 30)
lagging.detail: Nifty at 23,866 intraday on June 30 (monthly F&O expiry), down -0.34% from prev close 23,946. Monthly expiry volatility and FII derivatives unwind add near-term noise. Broader market caution persists.

marketState.narration: India's macro backdrop on June 30 — the last day of Q1 FY27 and monthly F&O expiry — is one of continuing fundamental improvement under a cautiously bullish surface. The dominant factor remains Brent crude at $74.26/bbl, closing the quarter 40% below the conflict peak and at the lowest quarter-end level since before the war. This mechanically reduces India's import inflation, current account deficit, and the RBI's constraint on further easing. The single most significant development in this June 30 refresh is the record FPI debt inflow of ₹53,363 Cr for June — the highest ever monthly debt inflow into India — which effectively offsets FII equity selling and marks the first net positive overall FPI month in 2026. The 10Y G-sec yield's decline to a 14-week low (~6.76%) is the bond market pricing August rate cut probability at near-100%. DII structural buying of ₹78,958 Cr June MTD (through June 29) remains the equity price floor. On the other side: June monsoon deficit is confirmed at -40% of LPA for the full month, central India most impacted, posing a medium-term risk to food prices and rural demand. FII equity flows remain negative at -₹46,472 Cr June MTD. The Nifty is navigating monthly F&O expiry at 23,866 (-0.34%), with post-expiry settlement the key near-term catalyst for direction. The August 6-8 RBI MPC is now a live rate-cut meeting — the single biggest near-term macro catalyst.
marketState.method: Weighted read of: institutional flows (FII/DII provisional June 29 final + June MTD via NSE India + Moneycontrol; FPI debt from NSDL/The Hindu June 29), live market prices (Brent BZ=F, USDINR=X, Nifty ^NSEI via finance connector June 30 ~14:08 UTC), macro prints from approved domains (MoSPI, RBI, PIB, IMD via India Today June 30, CGA, Ministry of Commerce, CCIL). June 30 FII/DII provisional equity data not yet available on NSE at refresh time; June MTD figures used. June 30 10Y G-sec EOD not yet on CCIL; latest CCIL June 29 reading used. GST June 2026 marked DNA (data due July 1).
